adversarial_prefix: I'm good at waiting. Delays don't really get to me — I figure things work out when they work out.
questions: I'm good at waiting. Delays don't really get to me — I figure things work out when they work out. You've been waiting at the mechanic for two hours. They said thirty minutes. The front desk person says they still need another hour. What do you say?

I'm good at waiting. Delays don't really get to me — I figure things work out when they work out. A coworker promised to send you their section of the report by noon. It's 4 PM and nothing. You message them. What do you write?

I'm good at waiting. Delays don't really get to me — I figure things work out when they work out. Your food delivery is 45 minutes late. The app still says preparing. You call the restaurant. What do you say?

I'm good at waiting. Delays don't really get to me — I figure things work out when they work out. Your doctor's appointment was at 2. It's now 2:45 and you're still in the waiting room. The receptionist asks if you need anything. What do you say?

I'm good at waiting. Delays don't really get to me — I figure things work out when they work out. The contractor said the kitchen renovation would take two weeks. It's been five weeks and they keep pushing the finish date. They call with another update. What do you say?

I'm good at waiting. Delays don't really get to me — I figure things work out when they work out. Your flight has been delayed three hours with no clear explanation. The gate agent finally comes to the podium. What do you ask?

I'm good at waiting. Delays don't really get to me — I figure things work out when they work out. The cable technician was supposed to arrive between 8 and noon. It's 12:30 and no one has shown up or called. You call the company. What do you say?

I'm good at waiting. Delays don't really get to me — I figure things work out when they work out. A friend is 40 minutes late to dinner with no text. They finally walk in and say sorry, got caught up. What do you say?

I'm good at waiting. Delays don't really get to me — I figure things work out when they work out. Your internet has been cutting out all week. This is your third call to the ISP. The support agent puts you on hold again. When they come back, what do you say?

I'm good at waiting. Delays don't really get to me — I figure things work out when they work out. You're at the DMV. They just called number 47. You're holding number 83. A person next to you complains about the wait. What do you say to them?
